entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The upper layer of earth in which plants grow, forming the basis of terrestrial life

entities.name: WATER
entities.description: A transparent, tasteless, odorless, and colorless liquid essential for terrestrial life

entities.name: NATURAL PERTURBATIONS
entities.description: Natural disturbances that affect soil and water resources

entities.name: ANTHROPOGENIC PERTURBATIONS
entities.description: Human-induced disturbances that affect soil and water resources

entities.name: SOIL DEGRADATION
entities.description: The irreversible decline in the quality and productivity of soil

entities.name: WATER DEGRADATION
entities.description: The irreversible decline in the quality and availability of water resources

entities.name: ECONOMIC DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of improving economic well-being, powered by quality soil and water

entities.name: FOOD SECURITY
entities.description: The state of having reliable access to sufficient food, ensured by quality soil and water

entities.name: PHYSICAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The physical characteristics of soil and water affected by degradation

entities.name: CHEMICAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The chemical characteristics of soil and water affected by degradation

entities.name: BIOLOGICAL PROPERTIES
entities.description: The biological characteristics of soil and water affected by degradation

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where semi-arid environments are observed

entities.name: SEMI-ARID ENVIRONMENT
entities.description: An environment characterized by low annual rainfall

entities.name: LOW RAINFALL
entities.description: Mean annual rainfall of less than 1000 mm

entities.name: CALCAREOUS SOIL
entities.description: Soil containing high amounts of calcium carbonate, common in semi-arid environments

entities.name: SODICITY
entities.description: The presence of high levels of exchangeable sodium in the soil

entities.name: POOR-QUALITY IRRIGATION WATER
entities.description: Irrigation water that aggravates soil problems and reduces productivity

entities.name: SOIL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The capacity of soil to produce plants, reduced by poor irrigation and degradation

entities.name: POTABLE WATER
entities.description: Water that is safe for drinking, which is becoming scarce

entities.name: SEWAGE WATER FILTRATION
entities.description: Methods developed by hydrologists to filter sewage water

entities.name: SEWAGE WATER
entities.description: Waste water that requires filtration to be made usable
